title: Gaiden chapter system — how to unlock optional maps
body: End any chapter with ≤15 units alive in your army to unlock the next Gaiden chapter. Gaiden chapters contain rare items and exclusive recruits — most importantly Wendell (7x), Midia & Astram (16x), and Xane & Tiki (19x). Plan your sacrifices: recruit Tier D units (Wrys, Castor, Dolph, Macellan, Roger, Matthis, Lorenz, Frey) as deliberate casualties when you need to drop below 16 units. Never waste stat-booster resources on them.

name: The Altean League
recruits: Marth, Jagen, Cain, Abel & Frey — Automatically, turn 1
warns: One unit must be left behind as a decoy at chapter end so Marth can escape — Frey is the recommended sacrifice; he has the worst long-term potential and rejoins in Ch. 18 underleveled

name: The Fight at Galder
recruits: Gordin — Automatically, turn 1 (Archer)

name: The Long Road
recruits: Wrys — Automatically (Cleric; Tier D, keep only for Gaiden unit-count padding)

name: Embarkation
recruits: Caeda — Automatically (Pegasus Knight; joins with Wing Spear — effective vs. armor and cavalry)
items: Wing Spear (Caeda joins with it — effective vs. armor and cavalry)

name: Marth Embarks
recruits: Draug — Automatically (Armor Knight; extremely low speed — reclass to Paladin to fix mobility)

name: The Pirates of Galder
recruits: Ogma — Automatically (Mercenary; best base stats of any early-game unit)
warns: Julian is your only thief — keep him alive and use him for every chest and door in the game

name: A Brush in the Teeth
recruits: Navarre — Talk with Caeda (enemy Myrmidon; blazing speed; talk before he walks into your units)
Lena — NPC ally (Cleric; keep her alive)
Julian — NPC ally; appears with Lena (Thief; your only unit for chests and doors)
Castor — Talk with Caeda (enemy Hunter; Tier D, sacrifice candidate)
warns: Lena and Julian start as NPC allies in danger — rush Caeda toward them to prevent deaths
Talk to Navarre with Caeda before he walks into your front line

name: Battle of the Pass
recruits: Dolph — Talk with Caeda (enemy Armor Knight; Tier D, sacrifice candidate)
Macellan — Talk with Caeda (enemy Armor Knight; Tier D, sacrifice candidate)
warns: Dolph and Macellan are armored enemies — have Caeda reach them before your combat units do

name: Champions of Aurelis
recruits: Roger — Talk with Caeda (enemy Cavalier; Tier D, sacrifice candidate)
Merric — Automatically (Mage; joins with Excalibur — effective vs. fliers)
Matthis — Talk with Lena or Caeda (enemy Cavalier; Lena's brother; Tier D)
items: Excalibur (Merric joins with it — effective vs. fliers)
warns: Roger and Matthis are enemies — have Caeda or Lena talk to them before your units attack

name: Fire Emblem
recruits: Bantu — Automatically (Manakete; Tier C — keep alive to recruit Tiki in Gaiden 19x)
warns: Keep Bantu alive for the ENTIRE game — he is the only way to recruit Tiki in Gaiden 19x. Never invest EXP in him, but never let him die.

name: Lefcandith Gauntlet
warns: End this chapter with ≤15 units alive to unlock Gaiden 7x — Wendell, a free pre-promoted Sage, joins there

name: Wendell's Prison
recruits: Wendell — Automatically (already promoted Sage; usable immediately; low growths)
warns: Only available if you ended Ch. 7 with ≤15 units alive

name: Port Warren
recruits: Hardin — Automatically (Cavalier; 100% HP growth and 60% Def growth — one of the best units in the game)

name: The Pyrathi Dragon
warns: Manakete boss — Marth's Rapier is not effective vs. manaketes; bring silver weapons or forge steel weapons for reliable damage

name: Princess Minerva
recruits: Maria — Rescue from the castle interior (Bishop; Minerva's sister; needed to recruit Minerva)
Minerva — Talk with Palla or Maria (enemy Dracoknight boss; outstanding pre-promote, joins promoted)
Palla — Automatically joins alongside Minerva (Pegasus Knight)
Catria — Automatically joins alongside Minerva (Pegasus Knight; better speed growth than Palla)
warns: Reach Maria inside the castle and rescue her before she is killed
Talk to Minerva with Palla or Maria — she will not join otherwise; she is the chapter boss

name: Knorda Market
recruits: Linde — Automatically (Mage; joins with Aura — effective vs. cavalry; best magic unit in the game)
items: Aura (Linde joins with it — effective vs. cavalry)

name: Land of Sorrow
recruits: Jeorge — Talk with any unit (already promoted Sniper; joins with Parthia — grants +5 Res when equipped)
Lorenz — Talk with Caeda (old General; Tier D, recruit only for unit-count management)
items: Parthia (Jeorge joins with it — unique bow, grants +5 Res when equipped)
warns: Lorenz is a sacrifice candidate — recruit him only to have a spare unit for Gaiden unlocks

name: An Oasis of Magic
warns: End this chapter with ≤15 units alive to unlock Gaiden 16x — Midia and Astram are rescued there; both are solid units

name: Khadein
recruits: Midia — Rescue the NPC prisoner (Paladin; reliable mounted unit)
Astram — Rescue the NPC prisoner (Hero; excellent base stats)
warns: Only available if you ended Ch. 15 with ≤15 units alive
Midia and Astram are prisoners in the map — reach them before they die

name: The Sable Order
recruits: Frey — Returns (Cavalier; separated since Prologue 1 and severely underleveled; Tier D, sacrifice candidate)
warns: End this chapter with ≤15 units alive to unlock Gaiden 19x — this is the most important gaiden (Xane and Tiki). Bantu MUST still be alive.
Frey returns with very low stats from missing the entire mid-game; useful only as a Gaiden-unlock sacrifice

name: The Dragon's Altar
recruits: Xane — Automatically (Chameleon; copies any deployed ally's class and stats each turn — unique utility)
Tiki — Have Bantu talk to her (Manakete; 100% HP growth; uses Divinestone; one of the best late-game units)
warns: Only available if you ended Ch. 18 with ≤15 units alive
Bantu must be alive and deployed — walk Bantu up to Tiki and have him talk to her to recruit her
Missing this gaiden means permanently missing Xane and Tiki, the two strongest late-game units

name: The Dragonkin Realm
warns: Medeus, the Shadow Dragon, can only be defeated by Marth wielding the Falchion — ensure Marth has it equipped for the final battle
